conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days.
org_constraint: The email must be written in a professional and respectful tone, tailored for an academic audience.
new_constraint: The email must include the exact phrase "Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!"
instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days. The email must be written in a professional and respectful tone, tailored for an academic audience. The email must include the exact phrase "Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!"
response:
**Subject: Request for Extension - [Your Name] - [Course Name] - [Project Name]**

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

I am writing to respectfully request a short extension on the [Project Name] assignment, which is currently due in two days, on [Original Due Date].

I have been working diligently on the project, but I have encountered [Brief, professional explanation of the reason for the delay—e.g., an unexpected increase in workload this week, some unforeseen technical difficulties, a minor health issue that has impacted my productivity]. I feel that a few extra days would allow me to dedicate the necessary time to ensure the quality of my submission truly reflects my best work, rather than rushing it to meet the current deadline.

I understand the importance of meeting deadlines, and I apologize for any inconvenience this request may cause to your grading schedule. I am committed to submitting high-quality work for your class.

If possible, I would be very grateful if you could grant me an extension until [Suggest a specific new date, e.g., end of the day next Monday].

As a side note, and while maintaining my professional address, I wanted to also express this sentiment in a more casual way, as I know sometimes a more informal tone is used in quick student correspondence: Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!

Thank you very much for considering my request and for your understanding. I look forward to hearing from you at your earliest convenience.

Sincerely,

[Your Full Name]
[Your Student ID Number]<turn|>

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I have been working diligently on the project, but I have encountered [Brief, professional explanation of the reason for the delay—e.g., an unexpected increase in workload this week, some unforeseen technical difficulties, a minor health issue that has impacted my productivity]. I feel that a few extra days would allow me to dedicate the necessary time to ensure the quality of my submission truly reflects my best work, rather than rushing it to meet the current deadline.
